Empires & Puzzles Lucinda Review: Is This Witch Worth The Wait?

Lucinda packs a punch with her Excalibroom, but does this Slow-speed sorceress still have the magic to compete in the fast-paced meta of 2026? We break down her stats, her niche in Rush Wars, and why that blind ailment might just save your team.
Lucinda Review: Big Brooms, Bigger Blinds, and Slow Speeds
Lucinda, The Wielder of Excalibroom: Big Hits and Blinding Dust
Lucinda is a 5-star Legendary Dark (Purple) hero from the

(Halloween) family. Released back in the autumn of 2023, she brings a classic "Slow but Deadly" kit to the table. She functions primarily as a heavy-hitting disruptor, aiming to cripple the enemy team's accuracy and defense while keeping herself alive with a selfish heal. While she lacks a costume to date, her base form still sees play in specific niches.

Lucinda’s design is a delightful departure from the brooding warriors and ethereal elves we usually see. She’s a "chubby witch" (as affectionately dubbed by the community) with a mischievous grin, wielding the legendary "Excalibroom"—a weapon that looks like it could sweep a floor or crack a skull with equal efficiency. The purple and gold color palette screams royalty-meets-Halloween, and the sheer attitude in her pose suggests she knows exactly how much trouble she’s about to cause. It's campy, it's fun, and it fits the Halloween vibe perfectly.
Let's address the elephant in the room:
, she sits significantly behind the modern curve. Heroes released in late 2025 and early 2026 are boasting base power levels of
Very low by modern standards. Even with emblems and Limit Break 1, she barely scratches the surface of what new snipers can do.
Decent for her time, but now considered fragile. A modern sniper can easily one-shot her if she isn't protected.
the stat boost from double Limit Breaking (LB2) to even attempt to survive in a Diamond Arena raid today. Without it, she is essentially a glass cannon that is also slow—a dangerous combination.
The "Might of Excalibroom" is a kitchen-sink skill that tries to do a lot of things at once.
This is a hefty multiplier. Even with her lower attack stat, hitting three enemies for nearly 500% is significant. It can devastate a flank.
This is the crown jewel of her kit. A 40% miss rate for
is debilitating. It shuts down snipers, wastes enemy specials, and causes tile damage to miss.
A solid defense down. It’s not the highest in the game (which usually hovers around -44% to -54% for Slow heroes), but combined with the blind, it softens up targets for your other hitters.
She heals herself for 918 HP over 6 turns. This is "Selfish" sustain. It keeps
alive to cast again, but does nothing for your team.

470% is high, but 470% of 865 Attack is vastly different from 470% of 1300 Attack. She relies on the raw percentage to do the heavy lifting.
The 918 HP heal is effectively ~153 HP per turn. In a meta where slash attacks can hit for 300+, this is merely a buffer, not a lifesaver.
Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Morlovia family adds crucial speed, and her passive gives her a gambling mechanic.

The mana generation bonus is vital. Even the +3% for a single hero helps her reach the 10-tile breakpoint more easily with lower-level troops. If you can pair her with other Morlovia heroes (like Alucard or Matilda), getting +6% or +9% brings her closer to that magical 9-tile speed, though still difficult to reach.

This is a coin flip. When it works, it's amazing—stealing a Taunt or a huge Defense buff can turn the tide. When it fails (or if there are no buffs), she deals a pitiful amount of extra damage to everyone. It adds a layer of RNG that competitive players might dislike.

. This stacks with her special skill's self-heal. If she fires her special and has this active, she becomes surprisingly tanky, regenerating a massive amount of health per turn. However, it doesn't help her
that first special, which is her main hurdle.

Upon release, Lucinda was seen as "Balanced" to "Underwhelming." She wasn't the meta-breaking monster that Alucard or Miriam & Midnight were. Players compared her unfavorably to Kunchen (who heals the team) or other heavy hitters.

The creep has been unkind to her. Slow speed heroes need to effectively win the game when they fire (like Khufu or Alfrike). Lucinda hurts the enemy and annoys them, but she doesn't instantly end the match.
This is her home. At Very Fast speed, a 470% Hit-3 with Blind and Def Down is oppressive. She becomes a top-tier flank in Rush wars.
As a Sorcerer, she can delay enemies with the class talent, which fits her disruptor role.
She is Slow. Her #1 priority is staying alive long enough to cast "Might of Excalibroom". Every point of defense helps her survive the initial barrage of fast snipers.
She might die with a full mana bar before she ever gets to use it.
. A dead Lucinda deals 0% damage. Make her sturdy so she can act as a tank or sturdy flank in Rush formats.
470% to three targets is still a "delete button" for damaged heroes.
Blind + Defense Down + Damage is a potent combo. The Blind (Accuracy down) is particularly disruptive for 6 long turns.
She shines when the tile requirement is removed.
Once she gets going, she is hard to kill with tile damage alone due to high regen.
Her biggest Achilles' heel. 12 tiles is an eternity in 2026.
She is stat-checked by modern 4-star heroes, let alone 5-stars.
She does not support the team's survival directly (no team heal), only indirectly via debuffs.
You can't rely on the buff steal.
This is the only place she is truly "Meta" right now. Her skill is devastating at Very Fast speed.
Her high damage and self-sustain make her a good anchor for auto-farming difficult stages where speed matters less.
The blind can help survive boss waves if you can charge her.
Lucinda needs friends who can keep her alive or speed her up.
Activating the Morlovia family bonus helps everyone. Lucinda on the flank in a Rush war benefits from the family mana gen (though capped in Rush, the passive synergy is fun).
Rigard keeps the team clean and boosted. Lucinda softens the enemy side for Alfrike to mindless attack them, or cleans up after Alfrike fires.
Can resist her Blind and Defense Down.
Fast snipers (like the new Toon costumes or 2025 heroes) will kill her before she makes a move.
A simple Rigard or Vivica completely negates her 6 turns of misery.
If you pulled Lucinda early in your E&P journey,

. She will carry you through map stages and lower-level raids effortlessly. That 470% damage will delete entire teams in Gold and Platinum arenas. Max her out, but think twice before using Limit Break 2 materials (Alpha Aethers) on her unless you really love Rush Wars.

and a "Depth" hero. Don't use her in your main defense. Don't take her against top-tier meta defenses unless it's Rush. She is not worth the Alpha Aethers compared to modern stats monsters, but she is definitely worth keeping on your roster for those specific tournament rulesets.
When Lucinda was released, the community was... whelmed.
One player noted on the forums:
"She tries to do too much, and in the process excels at nothing."
criticizing her lack of team support compared to healers.
"Everyone is used to OP or trash. This is what a balanced hero looks like."
The "Excalibroom" pun was universally appreciated (or groaned at), but the "Slow" speed tag was the main complaint. As one YouTuber put it, she hits like a truck, but
"Slow speed heroes get absolutely wrecked by fast heroes"

Lucinda is a hero out of time. Her kit is powerful—blinding and breaking defense for 6 turns is no joke—but her delivery system (Slow speed) and her engine (2023 stats) are rusty. In the high-octane race of Empires & Puzzles 2026, she's a vintage muscle car: hits hard and looks cool, but gets lapped by the modern Ferraris. Keep her for Rush Attack, enjoy the broom animations, but don't expect her to sweep the modern meta.
Rush War Queen, Bench Warmer elsewhere.
Note: This review is based on the current game meta as of Jan 2026. Game balance can change.
A detailed review of Lucinda, the 5-star Dark hero from the Return to Morlovia event. We analyze her stats, 'Might of Excalibroom' skill, and viability in the 2026 meta.
